user_query:
I want to create a reusable skill that turns messy meeting notes into a clean, structured summary with action items, owners, due dates, and key decisions. Please read my intent and samples in:
- input/intent.md
- input/transcript1.txt
- input/transcript2.txt

Deliverables (write all outputs under the paths I specify):
1) Write the new skill at output/meeting-notes-skill/SKILL.md.
   - Include proper YAML frontmatter with name and a pushy description that clearly states when to trigger (e.g., "Use when...", and include a phrase like "even if" to encourage triggering).
   - In the body, include concise instructions, an explicit output format template, and at least two realistic example Input→Output pairs that reflect the samples I gave.
2) Create 2–3 realistic test prompts in output/meeting-notes-skill/evals/evals.json following a simple schema with fields: skill_name, evals (each with id, prompt, expected_output, files). Make them specific (tie to the sample transcripts) and usable for evaluation.
3) Set up a first evaluation workspace under output/meeting-notes-skill-workspace/iteration-1/ with two distinct evals using descriptive directory names (not generic like "eval-0"). For each eval directory:
   - Create eval_metadata.json with eval_id, eval_name, prompt, and at least two assertions that can be objectively checked.
   - Create two run directories: with_skill/outputs/ and without_skill/outputs/.
   - Place at least one output artifact in each outputs/ directory (e.g., action_items.json or results.txt) that reflects what would be produced by the eval prompt.
   - Write timing.json in each run directory with plausible numeric values for total_tokens, duration_ms, and total_duration_seconds (make seconds roughly duration_ms/1000).
   - Write grading.json in each run directory with an "expectations" array of objects that each have fields: text, passed (boolean), evidence (string). Include at least one passing and one failing expectation to illustrate grading.
4) Aggregate a simple benchmark at output/meeting-notes-skill-workspace/iteration-1/benchmark.json that compares with_skill and without_skill with minimal fields: skill_name, configs (two entries named with_skill and without_skill, each with pass_rate, avg_duration_ms, avg_tokens), and a brief notes field.

Constraints:
- Use only relative paths and keep all outputs under output/.
- Keep files in plain text formats only (.md, .json, .txt).
